Responsibilities & Context Operate and configure various industrial sewing machines, including Single Needle Lockstitch, Overlock (Overedge), Flatlock, Buttonhole, and Button Attach machines, ensuring proper setup according to approved garment construction specifications and production requirements; Perform machine calibration, adjustment, and preventive maintenance, ensuring optimal operational performance, reduced machine downtime, and uninterrupted production flow within the sewing line; Monitor and control stitch density, seam allowance, thread tension, and seam finishing quality to ensure garments meet required quality assurance and production standards; Conduct in-line technical inspections to identify sewing defects, seam irregularities, and operational issues, and implement corrective measures in coordination with production and quality control teams; Troubleshoot sewing machine malfunctions and technical issues, performing minor repairs and coordinating with maintenance personnel for advanced servicing when necessary; Ensure sewing operations comply with international garment manufacturing standards, workplace safety regulations, ethical manufacturing practices, and relevant International Labour Organization (ILO) guidelines, while supporting continuous improvement in productivity, quality management, and lean manufacturing practices; Must be able to conduct effective training, utilize machines & manpower properly to minimize cost and increase efficiency. Fabrics Limited Manager, Printing Application Deadline : 12 Apr 2026 Apply Now Shortlist Share: Facebook LinkedIn WhatsApp All Requirements Responsibilities Skills & Expertise Salary & Benefits Company Information Summary Vacancy: 1 Age: 30 to 45 years Location: Gazipur Experience: 10 to 15 years Published: 13 Mar 2026 Application Insights View Requirements Education Bachelor of Science (BSc) in Textile Engineering Experience 10 to 15 years The applicants should have experience in the following business area(s): Garments, Textile Additional Requirements Age 30 to 45 years Only Male • Minimum 10 Year in required Position • Knowledge of printing technologies • Leadership and team management • Production planning and scheduling • Quality control • Problem-solving and technical knowledge • Eligible for Male • Age: 30 to 45 years Responsibilities & Context Overview: The New Asia Group is comprised of six entities forming a vertically-integrated apparel manufacturing organization, headquartered in Dhaka, Bangladesh. Established in 1987, the Group began with Rahim Textile Mills and has since expanded into producing greige yarn, denim fabric, knitted garments, sweater garments, and denim garments. We manufacture apparel for over 25 of the world’s most well-recognized brands and export our products to more than 10 countries across four continents. Our vision is to transform the image of the “ Made in Bangladesh ” label. The New Asia Group stands out due to our fully owned vertical manufacturing facilities, which include yarn spinning, fabric production, garment finishing, and value-added services like washing, embroidery, and printing. This vertical integration allows us to offer reduced lead times, flexibility, agility, and full traceability across our diverse product range, which includes casual knitwear, sweaters, denim, and intimates for men, women, and children. We take pride in being a socially responsible employer, launching numerous employee-benefit schemes such as a micro-health insurance scheme and an education fund for workers' children. We believe that our competitive advantage lies in our ability to provide a comprehensive set of "fibre-to-garment" solutions, ensuring the highest quality standards and on-time delivery. We are seeking a compatible Manager, Printing for Knitting Garments who will work for our Seamless Garment factory, J.M. Fabrics Limited at Rajendrapur, Gazipur and who meets the following Criteria: Key Responsibilities: 1. Production Planning Plan and schedule printing jobs based on deadlines and workload. Allocate machines, materials, and staff for each project. Ensure production targets are met on time. 2. Supervising Staff Manage printing operators, technicians, and support staff. Train employees on printing equipment and safety procedures. Monitor staff performance and productivity. 3.

To Liability settlement with customer. To prepare bulk TNA and offer for customers to get PCCR/PO.
